howItWorks.intro: This emergency radio range calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Line-of-Sight = 1.23 x sqrt(Antenna Height in ft)
Practical Range = Base Range x Terrain Factor
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How far can a 5-watt radio reach?
faq.answer: A 5-watt UHF/VHF handheld typically reaches 3-5 miles in suburban terrain, 1-2 miles in urban areas, and 10-15 miles with line of sight from elevation. Advertised ranges of 25-35 miles are theoretical maximums achievable only under ideal conditions.

faq.question: What is the best emergency radio frequency?
faq.answer: For most emergencies: GMRS (requires license) offers 5W power and repeater access. FRS is license-free but limited to 0.5W. Ham radio (VHF/UHF with Technician license) gives the most flexibility. Channel 9 on CB (27 MHz) is the traditional emergency channel.

faq.question: Is the Emergency Radio Range Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What transmit power should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate transmit power value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Emergency Radio Range Calculator?
sections.body: The Emergency Radio Range Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your transmit power, frequency band, terrain, you get instant results including theoretical range, practical range, line-of-sight horizon. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting theoretical range right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your transmit power and need to find the right theoretical range. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Transmit Power (watts)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your transmit power — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: frequency band, terrain, antenna height. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your theoretical range, practical range, line-of-sight horizon. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your transmit power before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both theoretical range and practical range — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your antenna height, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
